title: Glucose testing
text: Blood glucose should be measured with a blood sample, finger-stick glucometer, continuous glucose monitor, or lab test. If no glucose value is available, A1C or fasting plasma glucose testing is more appropriate than guessing.

title: Blood pressure follow-up
text: Elevated blood pressure should be confirmed with repeat measurements and reviewed with a clinician, especially when systolic pressure is persistently around 130 to 140 mmHg or higher.

title: BMI context
text: BMI is calculated from height and weight. A BMI around 25 or higher is considered overweight, and around 30 or higher is considered obese, but BMI should be interpreted alongside other clinical context.

title: High risk clinical review
text: High predicted cardiometabolic risk should trigger clinician review, confirmation of key measurements, medication and history review, and a care plan focused on modifiable risk factors.

title: Lifestyle foundations
text: General cardiometabolic risk reduction commonly includes balanced nutrition, regular physical activity, sleep support, smoking avoidance, blood pressure control, and appropriate follow-up.
